title: adjust string to use default machine name instead of helper path in helper message
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?


this fixes #20579 

```release-note
fixed a helper message to display the right command to execute
```

comments.body: Thanks! I think you need to add [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
Oops misclick, did not mean to close the PR.
yes, was just inspecting the pipeline that failed and added it now
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

@RiRa12621: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20580#issuecomment-1793002769):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
There is a lint test failure in the results there that looks legit (not all of the print statements are passed two args)

indeed. 
after https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20580#discussion_r1381886991 i didn't look properly.

the string that's used where the original problem occured is used by `fmt.Printf(fmtString, helper, suffix)` so string and two args and the format. the other string is (where the linter fails now) is actually used by *F*printf --> `fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, fmtString, suffix)`. Adjusting now.
